project: MySQL to BigQuery Data Ingestion Pipeline
commit_message: feat: SCRUM-123 — This pipeline ingests the MySQL `agentichub.employees` table (hosted on Cloud SQ
pipeline_summary: This pipeline ingests the MySQL `agentichub.employees` table (hosted on Cloud SQL instance `verizon-data:us-central1:mysql-druid-metadatastore` at `34.70.79.163:3306`) into the BigQuery dataset `verizon_data_deah` using Cloud Composer 2 as the orchestrator. Two DAGs are implemented: (1) `employees_full_load` — a manually triggered, one-time historical full load that exports all rows via the Cloud SQL Admin API to GCS (`gs://verizon-data-etl-staging/employees/full/`), loads into a transient buffer table `employees_staging` via a BigQuery Native Load Job, and MERGEs into the permanent partitioned staging table `stg_employees`; (2) `employees_incremental_load` — a scheduled hourly DAG (`0 * * * *`) that applies a watermark-delta filter (`updated_date > last_watermark` or `created_date > last_watermark` as fallback, or PK-delta as secondary fallback) to export only new/changed rows to GCS (`gs://verizon-data-etl-staging/employees/incremental/`), loads into `employees_staging`, and MERGEs into `stg_employees`. Both DAGs enforce pre-load schema compatibility checks, post-load row-count validation, null constraint violation scans, watermark advancement, and audit log writes to `etl_audit_log`. The target table `stg_employees` is partitioned by `updated_date` (DAY granularity) and clustered on `[department_id, status, job_id]`. Five BigQuery control/support tables are provisioned: `stg_employees`, `employees_staging`, `etl_watermark`, `etl_audit_log`, and `etl_control`. MySQL credentials are injected via environment variable at runtime. Cloud Monitoring alerting and a Looker Studio dashboard on `etl_audit_log` provide operational visibility.
